# FidelityFX integration notes

Official upstream source is pinned, unmodified algorithm code:

- FSR1: `GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-FSR` commit `a21ffb8f6c13233ba336352bdff293894c706575`, header version `1.20210629`.
- CAS: `GPUOpen-Effects/FidelityFX-CAS` commit `9fabcc9a2c45f958aff55ddfda337e74ef894b7f`, header version `1.20190610`.

`ffx_a.h`, `ffx_fsr1.h`, and `ffx_cas.h` are official files. The official Vulkan sample shaders and constant-setup source are retained as references. Project adapters live under `shaders/` and only define Vulkan bindings, official callback functions, dispatch bounds, and image stores.

EASU uses `FsrEasuF`; FSR sharpening uses `FsrRcasF`; standalone sharpening uses `CasFilter`. Constants are generated by the official CPU functions `FsrEasuCon`, `FsrRcasCon`, and `CasSetup` in `FidelityFxConstants.cpp`.

The user-facing RCAS strength is mapped to official attenuation stops as `stops = 2 * (1 - strength)`. The official function then converts stops exponentially with `exp2(-stops)`, so the displayed strength is not passed off as a linear filter coefficient. CAS strength is the official `CasSetup` 0-to-1 control.
